Ingrown hairs 
Ingrown hairs cause bumps, which are often itchy, where a hair has grown back into the skin. There are things you can do to treat and prevent them and in most cases you will not need to a see a GP for treatment. 
Symptoms of an ingrown hair 
Credit: SCIENCE PHOTO LIBRARY https://www.sciencephoto.com/media/257011/view
Ingrown hairs can look like raised, itchy bumps on the skin.
On white skin the bumps may look red. Redness may be harder to see on black or brown skin, but they may look a different colour to the surrounding skin.
Sometimes you can see a hair trapped under the skin.
You may be more likely to get ingrown hairs if you have coarse or curly hair.
Credit: Ocskay Mark / Alamy Stock Photo https://www.alamy.com/close-up-picture-of-folliculitis-on-female-skin-hands-squeezing-the-infected-follicle-painful-image245715366.html?pv=1&stamp=2&imageid=AFC254A6-771A-4A0D-A603-C2AE5A436D45&p=383112&n=0&orientation=0&pn=1&searchtype=0&IsFromSearch=1&srch=foo%3dbar%26st%3d0%26pn%3d1%26ps%3d100%26sortby%3d2%26resultview%3dsortbyPopular%26npgs%3d0%26qt%3dT7N8CP%26qt_raw%3dT7N8CP%26lic%3d3%26mr%3d0%26pr%3d0%26ot%3d0%26creative%3d%26ag%3
